sanskrit:
सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत।
ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत॥
transliteration: sattvaṃ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata | jñānam āvṛtya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta ||
literal_meaning: Goodness binds to happiness, passion to action, O Bhārata; ignorance veils knowledge and binds to delusion.
contextual_meaning: Each guṇa creates its own psychological gravity: sattva offers calm joy, rajas hooks us to outcomes, tamas traps us in foggy apathy.
psychological_state: Triadic mood-state capture: serene flow, restless striving, or dull dissociation.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m.—some posts uplift, some ignite craving, some leave you hollow and foggy.
anchor_text: Notice which mood—clear joy, restless drive, or dull fog—has captured you right now.
